title: Shadows of Brimstone - Weird West Interlude
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ar-bXQYvUhs

Daniel (@dungeondive) takes a Shadows of Brimstone-adjacent detour into weird western fiction, spotlighting two finds that any Brimstone fan should have on their radar.

First up is Richard Brautigan's *The Hawkline Monster: A Gothic Western* — which Daniel calls the single best weird western he has ever read, "by orders of magnitude." Brautigan, one of the last Beat authors (best known for *Trout Fishing in America*), delivers a punchy, jazzy, punk-rock novel with chapters so short some are a single paragraph. Daniel reads a passage involving two gunslingers who politely ask a deputy sheriff to please die after shooting him ten times — a scene that captures the book's tone of deadpan absurdity meeting genuine menace.

Second is the Splatter Western series from Death's Head Press — eight volumes of underground weird-west horror fiction whose covers could pass for Shadows of Brimstone box art. Titles include *The Magpie Coffin*, *A Savage Breed*, *The Night Silver River Run Red*, and the nearly 500-page *Hunger on the Chisholm Trail*. Daniel hasn't read them yet but bought the set on sight, noting they feature the same east-meets-west, Cthulhu-meets-church sensibility that makes Brimstone's theme sing.

----

What's your favourite weird western — book, film, or game?
